summary: The Phase 9A follow-up diff addresses five medium findings from the first review, all correctly implemented per the diff evidence: (1) requeue recovery now requires the referenced run-local job to still be "queued", (2) run-once mirrors already-terminal referenced job status instead of recording spurious failures, (3) entry creation uses atomic mkdir(exist_ok=False) instead of check-then-act, with cleanup-on-failure, (4) static schema contracts now call Draft202012Validator.check_schema, and (5) state-authority/evidence docs clarify queue records are diagnostic-only coordination artifacts. New tests (terminal mirroring, requeue precondition, abandon clears claim, worker-id authorization, CLI command existence) provide good behavioral coverage. Two remaining medium risks concern lossy terminal-status mapping and unhandled load_job_payload exceptions in the run-once path. No high or critical risks were identified in the diff.

findings.title: Lossy non-succeeded terminal status mapping to queue entry "failed"
findings.evidence: In cross_run_queue_run_once, when try_claim_job returns None and the referenced job is terminal, the code maps terminal status as: `terminal_entry_status = ("succeeded" if terminal_job_status == "succeeded" else "failed")`. Any terminal status other than "succeeded" (e.g., "skipped", "abandoned", "cancelled") is collapsed to "failed". The TERMINAL_JOB_STATUSES set is not visible in the diff, so the full set of affected statuses cannot be confirmed. The test only exercises the succeeded -> succeeded path. A job intentionally skipped or abandoned by the owning run would appear as a queue "failure", mislabeling the audit trail and potentially misleading operators during diagnosis.
findings.recommendation: Either enumerate terminal statuses explicitly (map "succeeded" -> "succeeded", "skipped"/"abandoned" -> "skipped"/"abandoned", genuine failures -> "failed"), or record the raw terminal_job_status alongside the queue entry status so the original job terminal state is preserved in the entry details without being lost to a binary succeeded/failed collapse.

findings.title: Unhandled load_job_payload exceptions in run-once can strand claimed queue entries
findings.evidence: In cross_run_queue_run_once, when job_claim is None, the new code calls `current_job = load_job_payload(run_dir / "jobs" / claimed_entry["job_id"] / "job.json")` without local try/except. Before this fix, the None branch went directly to mark_cross_run_queue_entry_terminal(...), guaranteeing the entry was always marked terminal. The new intermediate load_job_payload call introduces a failure window: if the job file is concurrently deleted or corrupted between try_claim_job and load_job_payload, the exception propagates without releasing the queue claim or recording a terminal status. The entry remains stuck in "claimed" until manual operator recovery. The diff context does not show a surrounding try/finally that releases the claim on unexpected exceptions, and the state-authority.md doc only documents manual recovery for this scenario.
findings.recommendation: Wrap the load_job_payload call in a try/except that, on failure, either falls through to the existing mark_cross_run_queue_entry_terminal(failed) path (preserving the pre-fix guarantee that None always terminates the entry) or explicitly releases the queue claim and records a diagnostic failure. Alternatively, confirm the surrounding loop body has a finally block that releases the claim on any exception and cite that structure in the review evidence.

tested: Requeue precondition blocks when referenced job status != "queued" (diff + test_recover_requeue_requires_referenced_job_to_be_queued)
Already-terminal referenced job mirrors succeeded status and is skipped, not failed (diff + test_cross_run_queue_run_once_mirrors_already_terminal_referenced_job)
Atomic entry creation via entry_dir.mkdir(exist_ok=False) replacing check-then-act exists() (diff)
Draft202012Validator.check_schema added to static contract schema loop (diff)
Worker-id authorization: unauthorized worker returns None, authorized worker claims (test_worker_id_must_be_authorized_to_claim_queue_entry)
Abandon recovery records audit, sets status "abandoned", clears claim_owner, removes claim.lock (test_recover_abandon_records_audit_and_clears_claim)
CLI commands queue-cross-run-job and run-cross-run-queue appear in --help (test_phase9a_cross_run_queue_cli_commands_exist)
evidence.md documents queue files as control/audit artifacts not auto-indexed (diff)
state-authority.md documents queue leases are diagnostic-only, no auto-steal, manual recovery required (diff)
not_tested: Full unittest discover execution (verification summary claims 330 pass, 1 skip — not independently confirmed from diff)
Live smoke reproducibility (two owning runs, byte-identical state.json) — claimed in summary but not verifiable from diff
Whether shutil is imported (cleanup-on-failure path uses shutil.rmtree but no test triggers that path)
Full set of values in TERMINAL_JOB_STATUSES (not visible in diff; affects terminal mapping accuracy)
mark_cross_run_queue_entry_terminal signature accepts the new terminal_job_status parameter (referenced in new code but definition not in diff)
Whether cross_run_queue_entry_dir is used consistently by all readers/writers replacing the old cross_run_queue_entry_path (structural layout change to entries/<id>/entry.json)
Whether queue iteration handles entries whose directory exists but entry.json is absent after a crash mid-creation
Whether the run-once loop body has a finally/except that releases the queue claim on unexpected exceptions (only partial context visible)
Whether append_cross_run_queue_event is atomic (partial event line risk on crash)
residual_risks: Local filesystem atomicity only: mkdir-based claims and atomic writes are not proof for network shares or cloud queues — explicitly documented as a non-goal in Phase 9A but remains an architectural limitation for any future multi-machine deployment.
Crash between entry_dir.mkdir and write_json_atomic can leave a directory with no entry.json; queue iteration robustness for this state is not verifiable from the diff.

Stuck "claimed"/"running" queue entries require explicit manual recovery (no auto-steal/reap); operators must run recover_cross_run_queue_entry with confirm=True. This is by design but creates an operational burden if workers crash frequently.

The new load_job_payload calls in run-once and recover paths assume the referenced job.json exists and is well-formed at call time; a narrow TOCTOU window exists between try_claim_job/load and the status read.
If TERMINAL_JOB_STATUSES includes non-failure terminal states (skipped/abandoned), the current mapping to "failed" produces semantically inaccurate queue audit records.

shutil import for the entry-creation cleanup path is assumed present but not confirmed from the diff; if absent, the except-Exception block would raise NameError instead of cleaning up, though this only triggers on validation/write/event failures which may be rare.
